title: China confirms detention of two Japanese nationals for alleged rare earth smuggling
summary: Chinese authorities detained two employees of Japanese companies in Dalian, accused of violating customs laws and smuggling rare earth products; the Japanese government has requested explanations.

title: Torrential rains in Kyushu leave woman swept away and landslide warnings
summary: A woman was swept away by a river in Kumamoto and rescued 50 minutes later; authorities forecast linear rain bands in northern Kyushu with risk of landslides.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 600 points dragged down by tech stock sales
summary: The Nikkei closed down 613 yen, falling to 69,174 yen, after a temporary drop of more than 1,300 points due to selling of AI and semiconductor-related stocks.

title: Bill against destruction of national flag enters debate in parliament
summary: The Cabinet Committee of the House of Representatives began deliberating the bill "Damage to the National Flag," which penalizes destruction of the Japanese flag, sparking debate on its necessity and scope.

title: Former Special Public Prosecutor to be tried for abuse of authority in interrogations
summary: A former prosecutor from the Tokyo Special Public Prosecutors Office was brought to criminal trial for allegedly shouting at and pressuring a suspect during an interrogation, violating their rights.

title: Curler Sayaka Yoshimura retires from professional sports
summary: Fortius team member and Milan 2026 Olympic representative Sayaka Yoshimura announced her retirement to prioritize time with family.

title: Bank of Japan reiterates commitment to continue adjusting monetary policy
summary: The BOJ deputy governor highlighted the need to continue normalizing monetary policy, while the government was cautious about the pace of rate hikes.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school caused by teacher drying clothes in classroom
summary: A fire at an elementary school in Kita-ku, Tokyo, was caused by a teacher who left clothes drying in the preparation room; the teacher expressed regret and police are investigating for negligent arson.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo achieves historic record by scoring in six consecutive tournaments
summary: The Portuguese forward scored two goals in the European Championship, becoming the first footballer to score in six consecutive editions of the European Championship or World Cup.

title: Japanese municipalities implement support to reverse bookstore closures
summary: Facing the disappearance of bookstores in many localities, local governments and companies are developing financial support models and new store formats to maintain access to books.

title: Sharp and Hon Hai expand cooperation to develop AI infrastructure
summary: Sharp and its parent company Hon Hai announced a strategic alliance for AI server business and other fields, on the occasion of the tenth anniversary of the acquisition.

title: Three progressive candidates win Democratic primaries in New York
summary: In the House of Representatives primaries in New York, three left-wing candidates supported by Mayor Eric Adams secured victory, marking a leftward shift in the party.

title: Princesses Kiko and Kako attend art exhibition by artists with disabilities
summary: The Japanese princesses visited the international art award for people with disabilities, appreciating works from creators worldwide.

title: Japan proposes increasing residence fee for foreigners by up to 20 times
summary: The Immigration Services Agency presented a plan to raise the residence fee from 6,000 yen to between 10,000 and 75,000 yen, and the permanent residence fee from 10,000 to 200,000 yen.

title: North Korea commissions new 5,000-ton destroyer with possible nuclear capability
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw the launching ceremony of the destroyer 'Choe Hyon,' which analysts say may be designed to carry nuclear weapons.

title: Convenience stores in Japan install 'monster wolf' robots to scare off bears
summary: Amid increasing bear sightings in mountainous areas, FamilyMart stores in Gunma have installed wolf-shaped robots that emit lights and sounds to deter animals.

title: SoftBank president criticizes low stock valuation and proposes alliance with TEPCO
summary: At the shareholders' meeting, Masayoshi Son expressed dissatisfaction that the market value is half of its net assets and suggested collaborating with Tokyo Electric to boost AI data centers in Japan.

title: Yen weakens to near 162 per dollar, its lowest level in 39 years
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend, approaching 162 yen to the dollar, while traders take advantage of the stock rise to hedge currency exposure.

title: Nissan shareholders' meeting rejects reelection of outside director Nagai
summary: Outside director Nagai, a former Mizuho executive, failed to get the majority needed for reelection, receiving only 48% approval at Nissan's general shareholders' meeting.

title: Japanese national football team prepares for World Cup with new defensive tactics
summary: Coach Moriyasu and defender Taniguchi analyze strategies for the match against Sweden, while an analysis highlights the team's evolution as the 'strongest in history'.
